cdf7429d1e2e.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
cdf7429d1e2e.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
cdf7429d1e2e.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

d645b197e2a6.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
d645b197e2a6.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

dda2f393bdd0.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
dda2f393bdd0.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
dda2f393bdd0.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

0ec1633cadd6.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
0ec1633cadd6.description: Your 4.7/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P33.3). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
0ec1633cadd6.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

0b83846b9d01.title: Ranked #3 of 4 locally — peers are ahead
0b83846b9d01.description: At percentile 33.3, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

80db1e2082b3.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
80db1e2082b3.description: At 20 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
